ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn Aspect Ratio accepts a numeric ratio and leaves media fitting to consumer classes.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora adds validated named or tuple ratios and independently optional direct-media fitting without replacing child semantics.

visualSignature.summary: Optional fitted media uses a quiet workbench surface while the wrapper stays structurally neutral.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Contain or cover fitting applies only to direct image, video, canvas, and SVG children.

disabledBehavior.ui: No fitted-media surface signal is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Direct children retain their intrinsic sizing behavior.
disabledBehavior.events: No events are installed in either mode.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No role, name, description, or hidden accessibility output is added.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Static semantics and composed Storybook axe checks pass; current manual screen-reader and zoom records remain absent.

interactionEvidence.summary: Preset geometry, narrow reflow, RTL, and forced-colors composition are covered in Chromium; the required cross-browser and manual matrix is incomplete.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Manual assistive-technology and zoom evidence is not recorded.
Required browser and device coverage remains incomplete.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn has no catalogued intrinsic Center primitive; consumers normally compose flex utilities and arbitrary maximum widths.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora combines logical-axis centering with selectable semantic reading bounds that remain safe on narrow screens.

visualSignature.summary: Semantic content and prose rails align with the shared measured workbench geometry without adding a card surface.

optionalEnhancements.summary: A prose or content maximum bounds direct children while preserving the available narrow width.

disabledBehavior.ui: No component-owned maximum-width presentation is applied.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Children are bounded only by the available inline size.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: Center remains a neutral div with no additional accessibility output.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Neutral server semantics and composed axe coverage pass; manual screen-reader, text-zoom, and switch evidence remains unrecorded.

interactionEvidence.summary: Narrow reflow, RTL composition, focus preservation, and long content are covered in Chromium only.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn has no catalogued wrapping Cluster primitive; consumers normally assemble flex-wrap and gap utilities.

visualSignature.summary: Logical spacing and aligned action rails follow the measured Living Workbench structure.

optionalEnhancements.summary: The final wrapped child may use the remaining row only when explicitly selected.

disabledBehavior.ui: No child is visually expanded by Cluster.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The final child stays at its intrinsic width on the logical leading edge.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: DOM, reading, and focus order remain unchanged in both modes.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Neutral markup and composed axe coverage pass; manual screen-reader, speech, and switch review remains pending.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn has no catalogued safe-area and query-aware Container primitive; projects generally compose max-width and padding utilities.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora combines semantic width and gutter rails with independently optional safe-area mapping and anonymous inline-size containment.

visualSignature.summary: Semantic content rails and logical gutters provide the shared measured workbench geometry.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Logical gutters take the larger of the semantic spacing token and the matching physical safe-area inset.

disabledBehavior.ui: No safe-area-specific gutter adjustment is visible.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Padding uses only the selected semantic gutter.

optionalEnhancements.summary: The root can become an anonymous inline-size query container without reserving a global name.

disabledBehavior.behavior: The root does not establish an inline-size query context.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No roles or accessibility nodes are added in either mode.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Neutral markup and composed axe coverage pass; safe-area device and manual zoom evidence is incomplete.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn has no catalogued intrinsic Grid primitive; consumers normally author responsive utility breakpoints and list semantics separately.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora provides breakpoint-free auto-fit semantic minimums, native list roots, and optional equal intrinsic rows.

visualSignature.summary: Measured semantic gaps and intrinsic tracks align evidence regions without card-grid decoration.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Implicit row tracks can be equalized when comparable evidence needs aligned vertical rhythm.

disabledBehavior.ui: Rows use their ordinary content-sized tracks.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: Native root and child semantics are identical in both modes.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn has no catalogued Inline primitive; consumers normally compose flex, alignment, wrapping, and gap utilities.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora supplies logical alignment and a resilient adaptive-wrap default that remains explicitly disableable.

visualSignature.summary: Logical token gaps and aligned inline rails reinforce the measured workbench rhythm.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Localized actions and metadata wrap safely by default while one-line compositions may opt out.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn Resizable wraps react-resizable-panels for mouse, touch, and keyboard panel resizing.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora owns a dependency-light controlled or uncontrolled value model with localized separator values, RTL spatial input, collapse, and optional explicit touch controls.

visualSignature.summary: Literal workbench surfaces, ink controls, green grips, violet two-layer focus, restrained corners, and reduced-motion-safe state feedback form the family signature.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Adjacent decrement, collapse or restore, and increment controls offer non-drag 44-pixel targets.

disabledBehavior.ui: The complete adjacent button group is absent.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Pointer and keyboard separator resizing remain available; no step-button path exists.
disabledBehavior.events: No decrement, increment, collapse-button, or restore-button handlers are created.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: The group, button names, buttons, and their focus stops are absent.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Named separator values, keyboard paths, disabled output, axe, focus, and forced-colors checks pass; manual AT sessions remain absent.

interactionEvidence.summary: Keyboard, pointer capture, collapse and restore, touch controls, RTL arrows, controlled state, and forced colors have Chromium evidence; other engines and devices remain open.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Required manual screen-reader, speech, switch, and touch sessions are incomplete.
Firefox, WebKit, and real-device interaction evidence remains incomplete.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn Scroll Area augments native scrolling with cross-browser custom scrollbar composition.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora retains one browser-native scrollport and independently adds named keyboard-region semantics or scroll-chain containment only when requested.

visualSignature.summary: A literal canvas, crisp block-edge structure, green native scrollbar signal, and violet two-layer focus identify the workbench scrollport.

optionalEnhancements.summary: An independently meaningful overflow region may become a named keyboard stop.

api.enableWhen: focusable is true and one accessible name prop is supplied.

disabledBehavior.ui: No additional focus indicator or keyboard stop is present on the root.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The root remains a native scrollport reached through its focusable descendants.
disabledBehavior.events: No custom keyboard or scroll events are installed.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: role, accessible name, and tabIndex are all omitted.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Scroll chaining can be contained independently from focusability and naming.

disabledBehavior.events: No custom scroll events are installed.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Type-enforced naming, inert disabled output, axe, keyboard scroll, and forced-colors evidence pass in Chromium; manual Safari and screen-reader review remains open.

interactionEvidence.summary: Block and inline native overflow, keyboard scrolling, narrow composition, and RTL have focused Chromium evidence only.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Manual screen-reader, Safari keyboard, touch, and zoom evidence is incomplete.
Cross-browser native scrolling evidence is incomplete.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn Separator provides decorative or semantic horizontal and vertical separation.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora preserves native horizontal semantics, explicit vertical ARIA semantics, forced-colors visibility, and optional logical spacing without wrappers.

visualSignature.summary: Crisp semantic-token lines provide ink-like workbench structure with a system-color forced-colors mapping.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Tokenized breathing room follows logical block or inline direction without adding a wrapper.

api.enableWhen: spacing is sm, md, or lg.

disabledBehavior.ui: No component-owned margin surrounds the separator.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The native separator geometry and semantics remain unchanged.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: Decorative or semantic accessibility output is unaffected by spacing.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Native and ARIA separator modes, decorative removal, axe, and forced-colors visibility pass; manual accessibility-tree sessions remain absent.

interactionEvidence.summary: Horizontal, vertical, narrow, RTL, and forced-colors geometry are covered in Chromium; broader engine evidence is pending.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn presents multi-panel resizing through its Resizable panel-group composition rather than a separate persistent Split Pane contract.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora adds bounded multi-panel normalization, controlled or uncontrolled sizes, optional collapse, consumer-owned persistence, nested panes, RTL input, and container-driven sequential mode.

visualSignature.summary: Flush workbench regions, literal surfaces, green grips, violet two-layer focus, restrained corners, and reduced-motion-safe feedback create a coherent family identity.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Every boundary may expose adjacent decrement, collapse or restore, and increment controls.

disabledBehavior.ui: Every adjacent resize-control group is absent.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Named separator keyboard and pointer resizing remain available.
disabledBehavior.events: No button adjustment or button collapse handlers are created.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: All control groups, button names, buttons, and button focus stops are absent.

optionalEnhancements.summary: At a narrow container size, panels return to full-width DOM order and hidden handles cannot strand content.

disabledBehavior.ui: Panels retain the selected split orientation at narrow container widths.
disabledBehavior.behavior: No container-query sequential layout is applied.
disabledBehavior.events: No additional events exist in either mode.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: Panel and separator semantics remain unchanged; no responsive hiding rule is applied.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Named separators, regions, collapsed exposure, keyboard controls, axe, and forced-colors focus pass in Chromium; Risk Class 2 manual AT sessions remain missing.

interactionEvidence.summary: Controlled nested panes, keyboard, pointer, collapse and restore, persistence, narrow stacking, RTL, and forced colors have Chromium evidence; other engines and devices remain open.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Risk Class 2 manual accessibility sessions are incomplete.
Firefox, WebKit, and real-device evidence is incomplete.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn has no catalogued semantic Stack primitive; consumers normally combine flex-column and gap utilities with separate list markup.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora combines semantic structural roots, native list preservation, token rhythm, and optional DOM-free structural separators.

visualSignature.summary: Measured vertical rhythm and optional crisp ink rules create workbench structure without card wrappers.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Direct children can receive tokenized block-start rules without injected DOM or semantics.

disabledBehavior.ui: No component-owned rules or separator padding appear between children.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Only the selected Stack gap controls vertical rhythm.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No separator nodes, roles, or hidden text are emitted.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Native list and neutral-root semantics, forced-colors rules, and composed axe coverage pass; manual list and zoom sessions remain pending.

interactionEvidence.summary: Narrow reflow, semantic lists, focus order, RTL, and forced-colors composition have Chromium evidence.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn has no catalogued Sticky Region primitive; a common composition uses sticky positioning without measured focus-not-obscured clearance.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora measures localized sticky content and coordinates scroll padding and descendant scroll margins so keyboard focus remains unobscured.

visualSignature.summary: Literal sticky canvas, crisp ink boundary, shared sticky layer, and measured focus rails belong to the Living Workbench family.

optionalEnhancements.summary: ResizeObserver-backed content measurement reserves the correct logical focus clearance as text, zoom, or locale changes.

disabledBehavior.behavior: No content node is retained for measurement, no ResizeObserver is installed, and no measured custom property, scroll padding, or descendant scroll margin is applied.
disabledBehavior.events: No measurement observer callback is created.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No accessibility nodes are added in either mode; disabled mode removes only focus-scroll assistance.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Landmark opt-in, deterministic SSR size, focus geometry, axe, and hidden-offset removal pass; manual screen-reader and zoom sessions remain missing.

interactionEvidence.summary: Contained scroll, final-focus visibility, measurement, and disabled enhancement output have Chromium evidence; block-end, nested transforms, other engines, and devices remain open.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Manual screen-reader, zoom, and focus-not-obscured sessions are incomplete.
Cross-browser and nested-layout evidence is incomplete.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.
